food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — beef burger, large fries, diet cola

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'hamburger' class (FOODON_00002383). No specific class exists for a smoky beef burger combo meal; closest_parent match used. Meal components (fries, cola) are not individually coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Hamburger) is the closest defensible anchor. No specific code exists for a smoky beef burger upsized meal combo. Closest_parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for a smoky beef burger combo meal. SNOMED CT food coding is limited for fast food combo items.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (1 smoky beef burger + 1 large fries + 1 medium Coke Zero)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one smoky beef burger (approximately 200 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one medium Coke Zero (approximately 326 g / 326 ml), totalling approximately 680 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Smoky Beef Burger, Large Fries, Medium Coke Zero (component-based estimation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact combo meal. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by component-weighted averaging: (1) Smoky Beef Burger (~200 g) modelled on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Fast foods, hamburger, large, single patty, with condiments and vegetables' (FDC ID 173966); (2) Large French Fries (~154 g) modelled on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Fast foods, potato, french fried in vegetable oil' (FDC ID 172931); (3) Medium Coke Zero (~326 g) modelled on USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods 'Coca-Cola Zero Sugar' (FDC ID 2097512). Weighted average computed across total meal weight of 680 g. Values are estimates and should be verified against McDonald's Singapore official nutrition panel when available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fast food hamburger and french fries analogous estimates
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Smoky Beef Burger Upsized Meal. GI estimated as a weighted composite: hamburger bun / beef patty component GI ~61 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread-based burger bun analogue); large french fries GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, french fries entry); Coke Zero GI ~1 (no glycemic carbohydrates). Weighted composite GI ~63 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (63 × 150.3) / 100 ≈ 30.0 g GL, where carbohydrate per serving = 22.1 g/100 g × 680 g / 100 ≈ 150.3 g total carbohydrate in meal. This is an estimate; direct measurement is preferred.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~2108 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 2108 mg sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — and should request reduced-salt options or choose lower-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate load and medium glycemic index (~63) resulting in high glycemic load (~30 per meal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its high glycemic load of approximately 30 per serving, driven by the large french fries and burger bun; portion reduction, substituting fries with a side salad, and choosing Coke Zero over regular cola are recommended strategies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~16 g per meal), trans fat (~1.4 g per meal), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is high in saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with CVD or at elevated cardiovascular risk should limit consumption and consider grilled lower-fat alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Smoky Beef Burger Upsized Meal is a fast food combo meal served at McDonald's Singapore, available via field survey. It consists of one Smoky Beef Burger — a beef patty with smoky barbecue-style sauce, lettuce, tomato, and cheese in a sesame-seed bun — paired with one large portion of salted french fries and one medium Coke Zero (diet cola with no sugar). The burger patty is seasoned with smoky spices and grilled, delivering a savoury, slightly smoky flavour with a juicy texture. The fries are deep-fried and lightly salted, providing a crispy exterior and soft interior. Coke Zero contributes no calories or sugar. The meal is certified Halal. Nutritionally, the full meal (~680 g) delivers approximately 1190 kcal, 39 g protein, 49 g fat (including ~16 g saturated fat and ~1.4 g trans fat), 150 g carbohydrate, and ~2108 mg sodium. Per 100 g, the meal provides approximately 175 kcal, 5.8 g protein, 7.2 g fat, and 22.1 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at 63 (medium), with a high glycemic load of ~30 per serving, driven primarily by the large fries and burger bun. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load), and cardiovascular disease (elevated saturated and trans fat). It is classified as ultra-processed and is not suitable for regular consumption in therapeutic diets for any of these conditions. It is suitable for occasional consumption by healthy adults as part of a balanced diet.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact McDonald's Singapore combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fast food hamburger, french fries, and Coca-Cola Zero Sugar branded entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the component-level USDA sources are approved and defensible, but the exact McDonald's Singapore formulation may differ. Values should be verified against McDonald's Singapore official nutrition disclosure when available.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per-100 g weighted averages across the full meal (burger ~200 g + large fries ~154 g + medium Coke Zero ~326 g = ~680 g total). 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy fast food entries used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component sources used. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. 5. GI is an analogous estimate; direct measurement for this specific meal is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The meal is listed as Halal on field survey data, consistent with McDonald's Singapore Halal certification. 7. Trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g, ~1.4 g per meal) is present due to the deep-frying process for french fries; this value is estimated from USDA SR Legacy french fries data. 8. Coke Zero contributes negligible macronutrients (energy_kcal ~0, protein ~0, fat ~0, carbohydrate ~0 per 100 ml) and its inclusion in the weighted average dilutes per-100 g macro values relative to the burger and fries alone. 9. Food ID preserved from input: GD000138.
